ucs.title: [LLM] LAPSUS$/DEV-0537 MFA fatigue ('push bombing') culminating in successful Entra ID sign-in
ucs.description: Detects the LAPSUS$ signature initial-access pattern: an attacker armed with stolen credentials (purchased from an IAB or Redline-style stealer log) hammers a victim with repeated MFA push notifications until the user finally approves one, immediately followed by an interactive cloud sign-in from the attacker's IP. This is the exact tradecraft Microsoft (MSTIC) and Okta documented in the 2022 LAPSUS$ intrusions against Microsoft, Okta, NVIDIA and Uber.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count as MfaDenials values(Authentication.src) as DenyIPs min(_time) as FirstDeny max(_time) as LastDeny from datamodel=Authentication where (Authentication.signature_id IN ("500121","50074","50158","50097") OR Authentication.reason="MFA denied" OR Authentication.action="failure") by Authentication.user span=30m | rename Authentication.user as user | where MfaDenials >= 5 | join type=inner user [| tstats summariesonly=true count as Successes values(Authentication.src) as SuccessIPs min(_time) as SuccessTime from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.action="success" Authentication.signature_id="0" by Authentication.user span=30m | rename Authentication.user as user] | where SuccessTime >= LastDeny AND SuccessTime <= LastDeny + 1800 | eval IpDelta=if(mvfilter(match(SuccessIPs, mvjoin(DenyIPs,"|")))="","new_ip","same_ip") | table user FirstDeny LastDeny SuccessTime MfaDenials DenyIPs SuccessIPs IpDelta
ucs.kql:
// LAPSUS$ MFA fatigue → success — 5+ MFA denials in 30m followed by clean auth within 30m
let WindowFail = 30m;
let WindowSuccess = 30m;
let MfaErrorCodes = dynamic([500121, 50074, 50158, 50097, 50140]); // MFA denied / required / interrupted
let Bombing = AADSignInEventsBeta
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ErrorCode in (MfaErrorCodes)
    | summarize MfaDenials = count(),
                FailIPs   = make_set(IPAddress, 20),
                FailCountries = make_set(Country, 10),
                FirstFail = min(Timestamp),
                LastFail  = max(Timestamp)
              by AccountUpn, AccountObjectId, bin(Timestamp, WindowFail)
    | where MfaDenials >= 5;             // empirical bombing threshold (P99 legit user ≈ 3)
AADSignInEventsBeta
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ErrorCode == 0                      // successful sign-in
| where IsInteractive == true
| join kind=inner Bombing on AccountUpn
| where Timestamp between (LastFail .. LastFail + WindowSuccess)
| extend NewIp = iif(IPAddress in (FailIPs), "reused", "new_ip"),
         FailToSuccessSec = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, LastFail)
| project SuccessTime = Timestamp, AccountUpn, IPAddress, Country, City,
          Application, ClientAppUsed, UserAgent,
          MfaDenials, FailIPs, FailCountries, FirstFail, LastFail,
          FailToSuccessSec, NewIp, RiskLevelDuringSignIn
| order by SuccessTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// LAPSUS$ MFA fatigue → success — Sentinel SigninLogs equivalent
let WindowFail = 30m;
let WindowSuccess = 30m;
let MfaResultTypes = dynamic([500121, 50074, 50158, 50097, 50140]);
let Bombing = SigninLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where ResultType in (MfaResultTypes)
    | extend Country = tostring(parse_json(LocationDetails).countryOrRegion)
    | summarize MfaDenials = count(),
                FailIPs   = make_set(IPAddress, 20),
                FailCountries = make_set(Country, 10),
                FirstFail = min(TimeGenerated),
                LastFail  = max(TimeGenerated)
              by UserPrincipalName, UserId, bin(TimeGenerated, WindowFail)
    | where MfaDenials >= 5;
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ResultType == 0
| where IsInteractive == true
| join kind=inner Bombing on UserPrincipalName
| where TimeGenerated between (LastFail .. LastFail + WindowSuccess)
| extend Country = tostring(parse_json(LocationDetails).countryOrRegion),
         NewIp = iif(IPAddress in (FailIPs), "reused", "new_ip"),
         FailToSuccessSec = datetime_diff('second', TimeGenerated, LastFail)
| project SuccessTime = TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, IPAddress, Country,
          AppDisplayName, ClientAppUsed, UserAgent,
          MfaDenials, FailIPs, FailCountries, FirstFail, LastFail,
          FailToSuccessSec, NewIp, RiskLevelDuringSignIn, ConditionalAccessStatus
| order by SuccessTime desc
ucs.rationale: LAPSUS$/Strawberry Tempest is the e-crime crew that put MFA-fatigue on the map. MSTIC's DEV-0537 advisory (Mar 2022) specifically calls out 'session token replay and prompt bombing of MFA' followed by self-service password reset as the group's hallmark initial-access vector after they bought stolen creds from underground markets. The 5+ denials → success-from-new-IP pattern within ~30m is the temp

ucs.title: [LLM] LAPSUS$ post-takeover Global Administrator / privileged role self-elevation
ucs.description: After phishing/SIM-swapping a help-desk or IT identity, LAPSUS$ characteristically promotes their compromised account (or a freshly created cloud account) to Global Administrator, Privileged Role Administrator, or Application Administrator within minutes of the first interactive sign-in — they then use that role to disable defenders, exfiltrate source code, and launch destructive operations. This hunts the role-grant happening on a session that was itself anomalous (post-MFA-bombing, new IP, or new device).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count values(Change.object) as RolesGranted values(Change.object_attrs) as ChangeDetails values(Change.src) as InitiatorIPs from datamodel=Change where Change.action="modified" Change.change_type="AAA" (Change.object IN ("Global Administrator","Privileged Role Administrator","Application Administrator","User Access Administrator","Privileged Authentication Administrator","Cloud Application Administrator") OR Change.command IN ("Add member to role","Add eligible member to role","Add app role assignment to service principal")) by Change.user Change.dest _time span=5m | rename Change.user as initiator Change.dest as target | join type=inner initiator [| tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as FirstSignin values(Authentication.src) as SigninIPs from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.action="success" Authentication.app="AzureActiveDirectory" by Authentication.user | rename Authentication.user as initiator | where FirstSignin >= relative_time(now(),"-24h")] | eval ElevationDelaySec=_time-FirstSignin | where ElevationDelaySec < 7200 | table _time initiator target RolesGranted ElevationDelaySec InitiatorIPs SigninIPs ChangeDetails
ucs.kql:
// LAPSUS$ — privileged AAD role granted within 2h of the initiator's first sign-in this week
let HighPrivRoles = dynamic([
    "Global Administrator","Privileged Role Administrator",
    "Application Administrator","Cloud Application Administrator",
    "User Access Administrator","Privileged Authentication Administrator",
    "Helpdesk Administrator","Authentication Administrator"]);
let RoleGrants = CloudAppEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where Application == "Office 365"
    | where ActionType in~ ("Add member to role.","Add eligible member to role.",
                            "Add app role assignment to service principal.")
    | extend Raw       = parse_json(RawEventData),
             ActorUpn  = tostring(AccountDisplayName),
             InitiatorIp = tostring(IPAddress)
    | mv-apply Prop = Raw.ModifiedProperties on (
          where tostring(Prop.Name) == "Role.DisplayName"
          | extend RoleName = trim(@'"', tostring(Prop.NewValue)))
    | extend TargetUpn = tostring(Raw.Target[0].ID)
    | where RoleName in (HighPrivRoles)
    | project GrantTime = Timestamp, ActorUpn, InitiatorIp, RoleName,
              TargetUpn, AccountObjectId;
let ActorFirstSignin = AADSignInEventsBeta
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ErrorCode == 0 and IsInteractive == true
    | summarize FirstSignin = min(Timestamp),
                SigninIPs = make_set(IPAddress, 20),
                SigninCountries = make_set(Country, 10)
              by AccountUpn;
RoleGrants
| join kind=inner ActorFirstSignin on $left.ActorUpn == $right.AccountUpn
| extend ElevationDelaySec = datetime_diff('second', GrantTime, FirstSignin),
         IpReused = iif(InitiatorIp in (SigninIPs), "reused", "new_ip")
| where ElevationDelaySec between (0 .. 7200)              // within 2h of first signin
| project GrantTime, ActorUpn, TargetUpn, RoleName,
          ElevationDelaySec, InitiatorIp, IpReused, SigninIPs, SigninCountries
| order by GrantTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// LAPSUS$ — privileged AAD role granted within 2h of initiator's first sign-in (Sentinel)
let HighPrivRoles = dynamic([
    "Global Administrator","Privileged Role Administrator",
    "Application Administrator","Cloud Application Administrator",
    "User Access Administrator","Privileged Authentication Administrator",
    "Helpdesk Administrator","Authentication Administrator"]);
let RoleGrants = AuditLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where OperationName in ("Add member to role","Add eligible member to role",
                              "Add app role assignment to service principal")
    | where Result == "success"
    | extend RoleName  = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName),
             TargetUpn = tostring(TargetResources[1].userPrincipalName),
             ActorUpn  = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy)).user.userPrincipalName),
             ActorIp   = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy)).user.ipAddress)
    | where RoleName in (HighPrivRoles)
    | project GrantTime = TimeGenerated, ActorUpn, ActorIp, RoleName, TargetUpn;
let ActorFirstSignin = SigninLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where ResultType == 0 and IsInteractive == true
    | extend Country = tostring(parse_json(LocationDetails).countryOrRegion)
    | summarize FirstSignin = min(TimeGenerated),
                SigninIPs = make_set(IPAddress, 20),
                SigninCountries = make_set(Country, 10)
              by UserPrincipalName;
RoleGrants
| join kind=inner ActorFirstSignin on $left.ActorUpn == $right.UserPrincipalName
| extend ElevationDelaySec = datetime_diff('second', GrantTime, FirstSignin),
         IpReused = iif(ActorIp in (SigninIPs), "reused", "new_ip")
| where ElevationDelaySec between (0 .. 7200)
| project GrantTime, ActorUpn, TargetUpn, RoleName, ElevationDelaySec,
          ActorIp, IpReused, SigninIPs, SigninCountries
| order by GrantTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: LAPSUS$ Privileged Entra ID Role Self-Elevation
id: 4c9b8e72-a1d4-4f31-9b21-7c51f0e5ad71
status: experimental
description: Addition of a high-privilege Entra ID role (Global Administrator, Privileged Role Administrator, Application Administrator, etc.) — LAPSUS$/DEV-0537 trademark post-takeover step.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2022/03/22/dev-0537-criminal-actor-targeting-organizations-for-data-exfiltration-and-destruction/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/05
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.privilege_escalation
  - attack.t1098.003
  - attack.g1004
logsource:
  product: azure
  service: auditlogs
detection:
  selection_op:
    OperationName:
      - 'Add member to role'
      - 'Add eligible member to role'
  selection_role:
    TargetResources|contains:
      - 'Global Administrator'
      - 'Privileged Role Administrator'
      - 'Application Administrator'
      - 'Cloud Application Administrator'
      - 'User Access Administrator'
      - 'Privileged Authentication Administrator'
  selection_result:
    Result: success
  condition: all of selection_*
falsepositives:
  - Planned PIM activations / approved JIT elevation by IAM team
  - Tenant migrations and break-glass account onboarding
level: high
ucs.rationale: Per Microsoft's DEV-0537 / Strawberry Tempest write-up and the UNIT 42 LAPSUS$ analysis, after social-engineering an IT/help-desk account the group's next move is to grant themselves Global Administrator (or a related Tier-0 role) — they need the role to disable conditional access, modify cloud compute (T1578.x), exfil source from internal repos (T1213.003) and ultimately conduct destructive opera
